sentence_reviews.text: Continuous predictions are trained by penalizing distance from numerical targets.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Probabilistic Machine Learning: An Introduction, classical ML and statistics coverage; landing page lines 45-48.
sentence_reviews.notes: Squared, absolute, and robust regression losses penalize deviations from numerical targets.

sentence_reviews.text: The role of Regression losses (MSE, MAE, robust) is to connect Supervised learning, Statistics & experimental design to downstream capabilities including Linear models & generalized linear models, Decision trees, random forests & boosted trees.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: PML landing page lines 45-48; AIMA contents, Chapter 19 'Learning from Examples' and supervised learning topics as cited in existing reviews.
sentence_reviews.notes: Regression losses connect supervised/statistical prediction to models, but the exact downstream list and graph role are atlas synthesis.

sentence_reviews.text: substrate for Linear models & generalized linear models
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Probabilistic Machine Learning: An Introduction, classical ML/statistical method coverage, lines 45-48.
sentence_reviews.notes: Linear regression is directly trained with regression loss; GLMs use related numerical objectives.

sentence_reviews.text: substrate for Decision trees, random forests & boosted trees
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Probabilistic Machine Learning: An Introduction, classical ML method coverage, lines 45-48.
sentence_reviews.notes: Regression trees and boosting can use regression losses, but the inspected evidence does not directly state the exact relation.

sentence_reviews.text: A tractable objective is a surrogate, so optimizing it can improve the score while leaving the intended behavior unchanged or worse. Its direct dependencies include Supervised learning, Statistics & experimental design.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Probabilistic Machine Learning: An Introduction, statistics and ML foundations, lines 45-48.
sentence_reviews.notes: The dependency portion is plausible; the surrogate mismatch warning needs a more direct source.

sentence_reviews.text: Regression losses (MSE, MAE, robust) uses Supervised learning as a reusable ingredient in its defined operation.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: AIMA Chapter 19 supervised learning context as cited in existing reviews; PML landing page lines 45-48.
sentence_reviews.notes: Regression loss requires targets paired with predictions, matching supervised learning.

sentence_reviews.text: Regression losses (MSE, MAE, robust) uses Statistics & experimental design as a reusable ingredient in its defined operation.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: PML landing page lines 45-48 describe the ML foundation in statistics and probability.
sentence_reviews.notes: Regression losses are statistical estimation tools; experimental design specifically is broader than the direct evidence.

sentence_reviews.text: Regression losses (MSE, MAE, robust) uses Calculus & automatic differentiation theory as a reusable ingredient in its defined operation.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: PML landing page lines 35-40 mention code and computational implementations across chapters; optimization/calculus details are not directly in the inspected excerpt.
sentence_reviews.notes: Differentiable regression losses use calculus, but MAE is not differentiable at zero and the source location is indirect.

edge_reviews.notes: Targets are intrinsic to regression losses, so supervised learning is a core dependency.
Regression losses are statistical estimation objectives, though experimental design is broader.
Calculus is common for optimizing differentiable losses but not necessary to define all regression losses.
